rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim (cross-family judging eliminates self-preference bias) and gap statement (LLM-as-judge bias problem) need sharpening — currently understated relative to the empirical results presented.
rationale.R1.2: The inferential leap from '0.3-point gap between GPT-4 and Claude' to 'self-preference bias eliminated' is logically incomplete; the argument needs tighter bridging premises (e.g., expected gap under bias, null hypothesis framing).
rationale.R1.3: The central claims — Spearman correlations, per-system score gap, bias elimination — are directly load-bearing on the tables/numbers cited; any strengthening must keep these tightly coupled and not overstate what the numbers show.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation is 'make this stronger,' which is primarily a writing-quality and rhetorical-force task — sentence-level precision, paragraph punch, and persuasive structure are the primary levers available.
